title: Boat Rental in Piran: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Piran offers affordable boat rental on the Slovenian Adriatic, with day charters from as little as EUR 200 for a small motorboat from Marina Piran. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners to find the best deals.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Piran?

A half-day motorboat rental starts at around EUR 250, while a crewed day charter can exceed EUR 800. Prices vary by boat size, season, and duration. For budget travellers, shared tours cost from EUR 50 per person and often include a swim stop near the Strunjan cliffs.

For full transparency on pricing and availability, browse day charters in Piran on BoatHire24; you can compare verified boat listings in EUR.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Piran?

For boats under 4kW (about 5hp) no licence is needed; otherwise an ICC or equivalent is required. The operator on BoatHire24 confirms exact requirements for each boat. Most small motorboats up to 20ft require a licence, but you can hire a skipper for around EUR 100 per day.

Always reserve your boat at least a week in advance during July and August; the local bora wind can cancel trips, so ask the owner about weather guarantees.

Where to set off in Piran

Marina Piran is the main departure point, offering easy access and parking. Other popular spots include Portorož harbour, the fishing port of Izola, and the historic town of Koper. For a quick getaway, launch from Bernardin pier or head straight to the island of Strunjan.

Many charters also include a stop at the Seča salt pans or the nearby towns of Savudrija and Umag just over the Croatian border.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6m), best for couples or small families exploring nearby coves; up to 6 guests; from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Bowrider or speedboat, ideal for watersports and fast trips to the Debeli Rtič nature reserve; 4, 8 guests; from EUR 350 half-day. For the Axopar 28ft model, check the Limpopo charter page for detailed specs and pricing.
  3. Sailing yacht (10, 12m), perfect for a relaxed day with a skipper; 8, 10 guests; from EUR 600 full day.
  4. Catamaran (12, 15m), spacious and stable, great for groups or sunset cruises; 10, 12 guests; from EUR 800 full day.

Cost breakdown for Piran boat rentals

  • Shared group tour: EUR 50, 80 per person (3, 4 hours).
  • Small motorboat half-day: EUR 250, 350 (6 guests).
  • Catamaran full-day with skipper: EUR 800, 1,200 (12 guests).
  • Crewed yacht full-day: EUR 1,000, 1,500 (10 guests).

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 50, 1,500 per trip
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 people
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Piran

How can I reach Piran from Ljubljana?

Drive or take a direct bus from Ljubljana to Piran in about 1.5 hours; the bus station is a 15-minute walk from Marina Piran.

Are there any hidden fees when renting a boat in Piran?

Fuel, skipper, and cleaning are sometimes extra; always read the listing terms on BoatHire24 before booking.

What is the best month for a calm sea in Piran?

June and September offer warm weather and lighter winds, with fewer crowds than July and August.

Can I take the boat into Croatian waters?

Yes, but you must inform the owner and carry a passport; the border is only a few nautical miles from Piran.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Piran or list your own. If you own a boat in the region, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 trip. You can become a host and earn extra income during the peak season.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Piran and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
